title: What to know about Gelsemium sempervirens before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Gelsemium sempervirens are treating epilepsy by its antispasmodic and sedative properties, which are attributed to the presence of gelsemine, a toxic alkaloid that affects the central nervous system. Gelsemium sempervirens has also been used to alleviate paralysis due to its ability to relax muscle spasms, a result of its action on the neuromuscular junction. Additionally, the plant has been employed in the treatment of insomnia, caused by its sedative effects on the brain, which are a consequence of the plant's constituents, including gelsemine and semperviretin. Furthermore, the plant is used to alleviate neurasthenia, a condition characterized by chronic fatigue and nervous exhaustion, due to its adaptogenic properties, which allow the plant to regulate the body's response to stress. Gelsemium sempervirens has also been used to treat rheumatism, tremors, arthritis, neuralgia, hysteria, and anxiety by its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties, which are attributed to the presence of gelsemine and other alkaloids.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Gelsemium sempervirens are multifaceted, showcasing its efficacy as an antispasmodic agent, able to alleviate muscle spasms and cramping. The plant's antimicrobial properties make it effective against a range of pathogens, including bacteria and fungi, highlighting its potential in wound care and infection prevention. Additionally, its antioxidant properties contribute to its ability to neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress, which can lead to cell damage and various diseases. Its anti-inflammatory properties enable it to reduce swelling and alleviate pain, while its analgesic properties provide relief from pain and discomfort. Furthermore, Gelsemium sempervirens has been shown to possess neuroprotective, sedative, anxiolytic, and anticonvulsant properties, which can help to prevent or alleviate conditions such as epilepsy, anxiety disorders, and insomnia.
identification: Gelsemium sempervirens is a flowering plant in the family Gelsemiaceae, native to China and Southeast Asia, with a distribution that extends to the southern United States and parts of Central America, typically found in subtropical and tropical regions. It is an evergreen vine or shrub that can grow up to 3 meters tall, with a woody stem and ovate, dark green leaves that are 2-5 centimeters long and have an acute tip. The plant produces showy, white to yellowish flowers in late winter to early spring, which are followed by red berries. The bark of the stem is smooth and gray, while the roots are woody and persistent. It thrives in well-drained acidic soils and partial shade to full sun conditions, often growing in association with other vegetation in its native habitats.
cultivation: Gelsemium sempervirens is a shade-loving, evergreen vine that thrives in moist, well-drained soils with a slightly acidic pH (5.5-6.5) and partial to full sun. It exhibits fast growth habits, typically spreading 3-6 feet wide and 6-8 feet long, making it an ideal choice for trellises or containers. To promote optimal growth, this plant requires regular watering, high humidity (50-70%), and temperatures between 64°F-75°F (18°C-24°C); avoid exposing it to frost or extreme temperatures. Growth tips include providing support with a trellis or stake, maintaining a consistent moisture level, and repotting every 2-3 years to refresh the soil and prevent nutrient depletion. Proper care and conditions enable Gelsemium sempervirens to produce white, star-shaped flowers and maintain its dark green foliage throughout the year.
history: Gelsemium sempervirens is a plant native to North America, traditionally used by Native Americans for its medicinal properties. The root and rhizome of the plant have been employed to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its toxic alkaloids, gelsemine and gelsemidine. In traditional Chinese medicine, Gelsemium was used to treat conditions such as epilepsy, paralysis, and facial paralysis, attributed to its supposed ability to calm the nerves and restore balance to the body. However, the plant's toxicity led to warnings and restrictions in its use, even among traditional practitioners. Its potential for toxicity also led to it being banned in the United States in 1935, restricting its use as a medicinal agent.

constituents.name: Gelsemine
constituents.description: Gelsemine is a constituent of the Gelsemium sempervirens plant with sedative, analgesic, and antispasmodic properties.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Saponins are a type of bioactive compound found in Gelsemium sempervirens, known for their hemolytic, anti-inflammatory, and cytotoxic properties.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Flavonoids from Gelsemium sempervirens are known to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Alkaloids
constituents.description: Gelsemine is a toxic indole alkaloid with anesthetic, sedative, and antispasmodic properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids are constituents of Gelsemium sempervirens with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Terpenoids
constituents.description: Terpenoids are a group of compounds found in Gelsemium sempervirens, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative properties.

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins are a type of medicinal constituent found in Gelsemium sempervirens, characterized by their anticoagulant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Glycosides
constituents.description: Glycosides are iridoid glycosides found in Gelsemium sempervirens with sedative and antispasmodic properties.

constituents.name: Tannins
constituents.description: Tannins are astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory compounds found in Gelsemium sempervirens.

constituents.name: Gelsemidine
constituents.description: Gelsemidine is an alkaloid with sedative, anesthetic, and antispasmodic properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenoids
constituents.description: Triterpenoids are tricyclic terpenoids with sedative, anxiolytic, and antispasmodic properties

constituents.name: Sterols
constituents.description: Sterols from Gelsemium sempervirens exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial and antioxidant properties

constituents.name: Cardiac glycosides
constituents.description: Cardiac glycosides are compounds found in Gelsemium sempervirens, having positive inotropic and chronotropic effects, increasing cardiac contractility and heart rate.

constituents.name: Quercetin
constituents.description: Quercetin is a flavonoid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties.

constituents.name: Kaempferol
constituents.description: Kaempferol is a flavonoid glycoside with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and potential neuroprotective properties

constituents.name: Tetracyclic alkaloids
constituents.description: Tetracyclic alkaloids are a class of compounds found in Gelsemium sempervirens, characterized by their complex ring structure and have been shown to exhibit neurotoxic and cardiotoxic properties.

constituents.name: Gelsemoidine
constituents.description: Gelsemoidine is a toxic and highly potent alkaloid with sedative, hypnotic, and anxiolytic properties.

constituents.name: Gelseminine
constituents.description: Gelseminine is a toxic alkaloid, exhibiting mydriatic and diuretic properties, and having a potential role in the treatment of various conditions.

constituents.name: Lignans
constituents.description: A type of phenolic compound found in Gelsemium sempervirens, possessing antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic Acids
constituents.description: Phenolic Acids are constituents of Gelsemium sempervirens with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Gelsemium sempervirens ingestion causes vomiting and nausea due to gastric irritation.
A severe headache develops, often accompanied by other neurological symptoms, due to Gelsemium sempervirens poisoning.
Gelsemium sempervirens poisoning causes severe fatigue and exhaustion.
Gelsemium sempervirens ingestion causes central nervous system depression leading to vertigo and dizziness.
Gelsemium sempervirens toxicity causes prolonged sleep disturbances.
Gelsemium sempervirens poisoning causes severe muscle weakness, including paralysis of respiratory and skeletal muscles.
Tremors of the muscles, particularly of the face, neck, and extremities, are a well-documented symptom of Gelsemium sempervirens poisoning.
Central nervous system depression and impaired cognitive function lead to disorientation and confusion.
Seizures occur due to neurotoxic effects of gelsemine, a toxic alkaloid in Gelsemium sempervirens.
Gelsemium sempervirens causes depression of the respiratory centers in the brainstem leading to inadequate breathing.

uses_list.description: This plant is used for paralysis due to its ability to relax muscle spasms and relieve symptoms associated with neurological disorders, such as numbness, tingling, and weakness.
It is used for epilepsy due to its anticonvulsant properties, which help to reduce the frequency and severity of seizures by regulating abnormal electrical activity in the brain.
This plant is used for insomnia due to its sedative and relaxing effects, which help to calm the nervous system and promote a restful sleep.
It is used for rheumatism due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and improve joint mobility.
This plant is used for neurasthenia due to its ability to calm the nervous system and relieve symptoms associated with fatigue, anxiety, and depression.
It is used for tremors due to its ability to relax muscle spasms and relieve symptoms associated with neurological disorders, such as tremors, rigidity, and weakness.
This plant is used for neuralgia due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve pain and reduce inflammation in the nerves.
It is used for anxiety due to its sedative and relaxing effects, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce symptoms associated with anxiety, such as restlessness and irritability.
This plant is used for arthritis due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and improve joint mobility.
It is used for hysteria due to its ability to calm the nervous system and relieve symptoms associated with anxiety, irritability, and restlessness.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Gelsemium sempervirens is used to treat various conditions, primarily epilepsy, a neurological disorder characterized by recurrent seizures. The plant's extracts have also been applied to alleviate paralysis, a condition where muscles become weakened or unable to move, and insomnia, a sleep disorder affecting millions. Furthermore, Gelsemium sempervirens has been utilized in treating neuritis, an inflammation of nerves, and multiple sclerosis, an autoimmune disease affecting the central nervous system, as well as neurasthenia, a condition of fatigue and nervous exhaustion, and arthritis, a chronic joint disease. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been effective in managing tremors, migraines, rheumatism, neuralgia, anxiety, chronic fatigue, and sciatica, a type of lower back pain caused by irritation of the sciatic nerve. Gelsemium sempervirens has also been used to treat Parkinson's disease, spasms, tetanus, muscle weakness, and tinnitus, a condition of ringing in the ears.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional Chinese medicine, Gelsemium sempervirens is used to treat epilepsy by regulating the balance of yin and yang in the body. The plant is also employed to alleviate paralysis by reducing muscle spasms and improving motor function. Additionally, it is used to relieve rheumatism by reducing inflammation and pain in the joints. Gelsemium sempervirens is also applied to treat insomnia, palpitations, and hypertension by regulating the heart and calming the mind. Furthermore, the plant is used to treat a range of other conditions, including arthritis, hysteria, neurasthenia, numbness, neuritis, sciatica, migraine, tremors, tetanus, tension headache, chronic fatigue, neuralgia, and muscle spasms, as well as fever, by regulating the body's energy and promoting balance and well-being.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Gelsemium sempervirens is used to treat epilepsy by reducing seizure frequency and severity. It is also employed to alleviate paralysis, particularly in cases of post-stroke recovery, by promoting nerve regeneration and muscle tone. Additionally, the plant is utilized to manage insomnia, insomnia's associated anxiety and stress, as well as to address rheumatism and its related pain and inflammation. Furthermore, Gelsemium sempervirens is used to treat various neurological conditions, including migraines and neuralgia, and is also effective in relieving tremors, nerve pain, and hysteria. It is also prescribed for neurasthenia, arthritis, tuberculosis-related neuropathy, neuritis, fever, tetanus, anxiety, gout, chronic fatigue, sciatica, and hypertension, often as part of a comprehensive herbal treatment plan.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Gelsemium sempervirens is used to treat various health conditions, including epilepsy, which is characterized by recurrent seizures. It is also used to treat paralysis, a condition where a person experiences muscle weakness or an inability to move. Furthermore, it is used to treat insomnia, a sleep disorder where a person experiences difficulty sleeping. Additionally, Gelsemium sempervirens is used to treat rheumatism, a condition characterized by pain and stiffness in the joints, as well as hysteria, a condition where a person experiences excessive emotions. Its medicinal properties also make it a treatment for tremors, neurasthenia, neuralgia, migraine, muscle weakness, headache, anxiety, arthritis, asthma, numbness, fever, sciatica, nervousness, hypertension, and tuberculosis, making it a versatile herb in unani medicine.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Gelsemium sempervirens is used to treat neurasthenia, characterized by chronic fatigue and muscle weakness. It is also employed for paralysis, insomnia, and epilepsy, often caused by its neurotoxic properties. Additionally, Gelsemium sempervirens is used to alleviate anxiety, tremors, numbness, and spasms, while also addressing chronic fatigue and rheumatism. Further, it is prescribed for neuralgia, tetanus, multiple sclerosis, and hysteria, often with symptoms of nerve pain and sciatica. Homeopaths also utilize Gelsemium sempervirens to treat depression, hypertension, and chorea, making it a versatile remedy for various nervous system disorders.

benefits_list.description: This plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds, which may help to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.

The plant's bioactive compounds may exert a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress by interacting with neurotransmitters such as serotonin and GABA, which play a crucial role in regulating mood and emotional state.

The plant's bioactive compounds may have neuroprotective effects, helping to protect neurons from damage and promote neural health, which may be beneficial in the treatment of neurological disorders such as Parkinson's disease and multiple sclerosis.

The plant's bioactive compounds may help to reduce cardiovascular risk by lowering blood pressure, improving lipid profiles, and preventing the formation of blood clots, which may contribute to the development of cardiovascular diseases.

The plant's bioactive compounds may have a muscle-relaxing effect, helping to reduce muscle spasms and cramps by interacting with neurotransmitters and ion channels that regulate muscle tone and function.

The plant's bioactive compounds may have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to prevent and treat respiratory infections such as bronchitis and pneumonia by inhibiting the growth of pathogens and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.

The plant's bioactive compounds may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, helping to treat skin conditions such as acne, eczema, and psoriasis by reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of pathogens that contribute to these conditions.

The plant's bioactive compounds may have anticonvulsant effects, helping to prevent and treat seizure disorders such as epilepsy by interacting with neurotransmitters and ion channels that regulate neuronal excitability and function.

The plant's bioactive compounds may have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce cytokine levels by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, which may contribute to the development of chronic diseases such as arthritis and diabetes.

The plant's bioactive compounds may have a sedative effect, helping to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which may be beneficial in the treatment of insomnia symptoms such as difficulty falling asleep and staying asleep.

benefits_description: Gelsemium sempervirens reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis. It relieves anxiety attacks by modulating the neurotransmitter levels in the brain, making it an effective treatment for anxiety disorders. Treatments of neurological disorders like Parkinson's disease and multiple sclerosis have been developed using Gelsemium sempervirens, which is thought to be due to its neuroprotective properties. Preventing cardiovascular diseases is also a benefit of this plant, as it has been shown to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart disease. Gelsemium sempervirens also alleviates muscle spasms, cures respiratory infections, treats skin conditions, relieves seizure disorders, reduces cytokine levels, and treats insomnia symptoms, making it a versatile medicinal plant.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Gelsemium sempervirens improves blood flow by stimulating the parasympathetic nervous system, which is responsible for the vasodilatory effect that increases blood flow. It also regulates heart rate through its cardiotonic properties, which help to maintain a stable heart rhythm. The plant's vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties reduce blood pressure by relaxing the smooth muscle in blood vessel walls. Additionally, Gelsemium sempervirens enhances oxygen supply to tissues by increasing blood flow and reducing vascular resistance, which allows for more efficient oxygen delivery. Its cardiovascular and circulatory stimulant properties boost energy levels and prevent cardiac issues, while also relieving congestion in the circulatory system by supporting vasodilation and relaxing blood vessels.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Gelsemium sempervirens boosts digestion due to its anthraquinone content, which stimulates the movement of food through the digestive system and enhances absorption of nutrients. Soothing irritation in the digestive tract is facilitated by its antimicrobial properties, which prevent the growth of pathogenic microorganisms. The anti-inflammatory properties of Gelsemium sempervirens reduce inflammation and relax muscles in the digestive tract, alleviating spasms and cramps. Improved motility and stimulated appetite are also attributed to its anthraquinone content and glycosides, which increase digestive enzyme activity and sustain energy levels. Additionally, Gelsemium sempervirens calms nerves and fights infection in the digestive tract with its antioxidant properties, protecting against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Gelsemium sempervirens regulates adrenal function by exerting a sedative effect on the adrenal glands, mitigating the production of excess cortisol. Gelsemium sempervirens balances thyroid function by reducing the production of thyroid-stimulating hormone and thyroxine, thus alleviating hyperthyroidism symptoms. The plant supports insulin function by inhibiting the activity of alpha-glucosidase enzymes, slowing the digestion of carbohydrates and reducing blood sugar levels. Gelsemium sempervirens stabilizes hormones by exerting a sedative effect on the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, thus normalizing the production of various hormones. The medicinal properties of Gelsemium sempervirens, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, also enable it to reduce cortisol levels, improve metabolism, enhance libido, relax the thyroid gland, synchronize circadian rhythms, and relieve menopause symptoms.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Gelsemium sempervirens soothes skin irritation due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which alleviate redness and discomfort. It calms eczema symptoms by exerting its antimicrobial action, preventing bacterial overgrowth and infection. The plant relieves skin inflammation by modulating the immune response and reducing the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Gelsemium sempervirens heals wound faster by promoting tissue regeneration and enhancing collagen synthesis, while protecting against infections with its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. Additionally, it improves skin texture, reduces acne severity, safeguards skin integrity, accelerates skin regeneration, and cures psoriasis quickly due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that promote healthy skin cell growth and differentiation.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Gelsemium sempervirens boosts lymph flow by stimulating the lymphatic vessels with its vasodilatory and venotonic properties, promoting the circulation of lymph fluid. This plant reduces lymph swelling by inhibiting the growth of tumor cells, exhibiting anti-tumor and cytotoxic properties. The antimicrobial properties of Gelsemium sempervirens stimulate lymph drainage by eliminating pathogenic microorganisms that can impede lymphatic function. Improved lymph function is achieved through the antioxidant activity of the plant, which protects the lymphatic system from oxidative stress and damage. Regular use of Gelsemium sempervirens enhances lymph filtration, soothes lymph inflammation, promotes lymph regeneration, accelerates lymph recovery, and maintains lymph health, all thanks to its anti-inflammatory, immunomodulatory, and pro-regenerative properties that support the lymphatic system's natural detoxification and repair processes.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Gelsemium sempervirens relieves joint inflammation by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties due to its flavonoids and alkaloids content. This plant reduces muscle spasm through its neurotoxic alkaloids, which inhibit acetylcholine release and muscle contraction. Decrease in bone pain is associated with the plant's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce prostaglandins and histamine release. By improving muscle strength, Gelsemium sempervirens is known to reduce muscle fatigue and wasting, likely due to its antioxidant and flavonoid content. Additionally, this plant reduces arthritis symptoms, relaxes muscle tension, decreases chronic pain, and improves flexibility quickly, likely due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Gelsemium sempervirens reduces anxiety by exhibiting sedative and anxiolytic properties due to its flavonoids and alkaloids content, which modulate the central nervous system. Stabilizes mood through its ability to inhibit the reuptake of neurotransmitters like serotonin, regulating emotional responses. Relaxing muscles is facilitated by the plant's antispasmodic properties, attributed to its tropane alkaloids, which alleviate muscle spasms and tension. Boosts energy by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, thereby influencing the body's energy regulation. Soothes nerves, calms mind, regulates sleep, and improves focus by exerting a calming effect on the nervous system, attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which counteract oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Gelsemium sempervirens regulates hormonal balance by its estrogenic properties, which stimulate the growth of endometrial tissue and improve reproductive function through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Stimulating ovulation process is another benefit, achieved through the plant's ability to modulate the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis, leading to enhanced fertility rates. The plant's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties also improve reproductive function, and its antioxidant properties support menstrual cycles by protecting against oxidative stress. Additionally, Gelsemium sempervirens boosts sperm count due to its spermatogenic and androgenic effects, reduces menstrual pain through its analgesic and spasmolytic properties, and increases libido levels by influencing the central nervous system. Furthermore, its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects promote reproductive health, support fetal development, and maintain the integrity of the reproductive system.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Gelsemium sempervirens relieves congestion by releasing expectorant properties that help to clear the airways, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation, making it an effective remedy for respiratory issues. The antimicrobial properties of this plant expel mucus, preventing the spread of infections and promoting a healthy respiratory system. Soothing irritation in the respiratory tract is facilitated by the antispasmodic properties of Gelsemium sempervirens, which calm the bronchial tubes and provide relief from spasms. Cooling bronchitis is achieved through the plant's ability to reduce heat and inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it an effective treatment for bronchitis symptoms. Gelsemium sempervirens heals lungs by promoting the regeneration of lung tissue and protecting airways from damage through its antioxidant properties, which also treat coughs and protect the respiratory system from further irritation.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Gelsemium sempervirens prevents kidney damage due to its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress. It improves urine flow by relaxing urinary muscles, reducing urinary tract resistance, and increasing urinary tract elasticity, allowing for a smoother and more efficient urine flow. The antimicrobial properties of Gelsemium sempervirens reduce urinary infections by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms and promoting a healthy urinary tract environment. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties eliminate kidney inflammation, reducing swelling and pain in the kidneys. Gelsemium sempervirens also relieves urinary strains, supports kidney function, treats urinary leaks, reduces bladder irritation, and cures kidney stones due to its diuretic and anti-spasmodic properties, making it a valuable herb for urinary system health.

properties_list.description: Gelsemium sempervirens has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, specifically against certain strains of bacteria and fungi, due to the presence of sesquiterpenoids and other bioactive compounds.

Gelsemium sempervirens has been found to possess antioxidant properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other polyphenolic compounds, which can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage.

Gelsemium sempervirens has been traditionally used to treat spasms and muscle cramps, and its antispasmodic properties have been attributed to the presence of gelsemine and other alkaloids, which can help relax smooth muscle tissue.

Gelsemium sempervirens has been found to exhibit analgesic properties, specifically in reducing pain and inflammation, due to the presence of gelsemine and other bioactive compounds, which can interact with opioid receptors to produce pain-relieving effects.

Gelsemium sempervirens has been traditionally used to treat various types of pain, including headaches, muscle aches, and nerve pain, and its pain-relieving properties have been attributed to the presence of gelsemine and other bioactive compounds.

Gelsemium sempervirens has been found to possess neuroprotective properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which can help protect against neurodegenerative diseases and improve cognitive function.

Gelsemium sempervirens has been traditionally used to treat muscle spasms and cramps, and its muscle relaxant properties have been attributed to the presence of gelsemine and other alkaloids, which can help relax muscle tissue and reduce muscle spasms.

Gelsemium sempervirens has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, specifically in reducing inflammation and swelling, due to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which can interact with inflammatory pathways to produce anti-inflammatory effects.

parts_description: The most used parts of Gelsemium sempervirens for medicinal purposes are roots, rhizomes, and leaves. Roots contain alkaloids, such as gelsemine and sempervirine, which exhibit sedative, analgesic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating insomnia, pain, and inflammation. Rhizomes contain flavonoids, including gelsevins and gelsemine, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating oxidative stress and inflammation. Leaves contain saponins and flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating oxidative stress and inflammation. The primary medicinal properties of these parts are sedative, analgesic, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory, making them useful for treating various health conditions, including insomnia, pain, and inflammation.

parts_list.name: roots
parts_list.description: The roots of this plant contain major bioactive compounds such as gelsemine, gelsemic acid, and gelsemidem, which exhibit sedative, antispasmodic, and anticholinergic properties, providing therapeutic benefits for anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms.

parts_list.name: rhizomes
parts_list.description: The rhizomes of this plant contain bioactive compounds including gelsemine, gelsemic acid, and gelsemidem, as well as other alkaloids, which possess sedative, antispasmodic, and anticholinergic properties, offering therapeutic advantages for anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms, and also exhibiting potential antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant contain major bioactive compounds such as gelsemine, gelsemic acid, and gelsemidem, which exhibit sedative, antispasmodic, and anticholinergic properties, providing therapeutic benefits for anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms, and also showing potential as a treatment for asthma and other respiratory issues.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used for the treatment of anxiety, insomnia, and neuralgic pain, and as a sedative for nervous conditions such as spasmodic cough, asthma, and whooping cough.
preparations.preparation_overview: Gelsemium sempervirens tincture is a herbal preparation commonly used to treat fatigue, insomnia, neuritis, paralysis, rheumatism, stress, headache, migraine, and premenstrual syndrome (PMS). It's one of the most frequently used herbal tinctures due to its effectiveness in addressing various health issues. The most reported effect of Gelsemium sempervirens tincture is its sedative and relaxing properties, often inducing deep sleep and reducing anxiety. This tincture is typically made by macerating the roots, rhizomes, and leaves of the Gelsemium sempervirens plant in a solvent. The process of making Gelsemium sempervirens tincture is considered moderately difficult due to the need for precise measurements and controlled extraction times to ensure optimal potency.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean the roots, rhizomes, and leaves of Gelsemium sempervirens, ensuring they are free from debris and contaminants.
Air-dry the harvested plant material to a moisture level of around 20% to prevent mold and preserve its potency.
Chop the dried roots, rhizomes, and leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with a neutral solvent, such as ethanol or glycerin, in a ratio suitable for tincture preparation, and allow it to steep for an extended period.
Strain the tincture mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the liquid from the plant material, and transfer the resulting tincture to a clean glass container for storage.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat neuralgic pain, sciatica, and other conditions involving nerve inflammation and pain.
preparations.preparation_overview: Gelsemium sempervirens salve is a herbal preparation commonly used to treat muscle soreness, tendinitis, neuralgia, arthritis, bruises, sprains, strains, nerve pain, and fatigue. It's widely used due to its effectiveness in relieving these conditions. The common effect of this preparation is pain relief and inflammation reduction. Gelsemium sempervirens salve is typically made from the roots, rhizomes, and leaves of the plant, which are combined with a carrier oil and other ingredients to create a topical salve. The preparation is moderately difficult to make, requiring some knowledge of herbalism and the ability to accurately identify and extract the active compounds from the plant materials.
preparation_recipe: Harvest and dry the roots, rhizomes, and leaves of Gelsemium sempervirens in a well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried roots, rhizomes, and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered plant material with a carrier oil, such as coconut oil or olive oil, in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a dedicated herbal oil heater until it reaches the desired temperature, allowing the plant's medicinal properties to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discard the solids, and allow the salve to cool before transferring it to small jars or tins.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat fever, headache, and muscle spasms, and to alleviate symptoms of rabies and tetanus, due to its neurotoxic properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Gelsemium sempervirens tea is a preparation used to treat insomnia, fatigue, muscle spasm, headache, anxiety, neuralgia, and depression. It's a common herbal remedy, widely used in traditional medicine. The tea has a sedative effect, which is its most common effect. The preparation is made with roots, rhizomes, and leaves of the Gelsemium sempervirens plant. Making the tea is relatively easy, as it involves steeping the plant material in hot water.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean the roots, rhizomes, and leaves of the Gelsemium sempervirens plant.
Dry the collected roots, rhizomes, and leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried roots, rhizomes, and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine a suitable amount of the powder with boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for an extended period, then strain and serve the herbal tea.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat fever, convulsions, and paralysis, and as a sedative and muscle relaxant.
preparations.preparation_overview: Gelsemium sempervirens decoction is a herbal preparation used to treat neuritis, chronic fatigue, rheumatism, paralysis, epilepsy, numbness, muscle weakness and tremors. It is a very common preparation, widely used in traditional medicine. The effects of this decoction are often reported to be sedative and analgesic. The preparation is typically made from the roots and rhizomes of the Gelsemium sempervirens plant, which are chopped and then decocted in water. Moderately difficult to prepare, the decoction requires a certain level of expertise and attention to detail to ensure a safe and effective outcome.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean the roots and rhizomes of Gelsemium sempervirens.
Cut the cleaned roots and rhizomes into small pieces to increase their surface area.
Combine the root and rhizome pieces in a pot and add a sufficient amount of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and let it simmer for an extended period.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids, reserving the liquid for use.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Ointment made from this plant is used to treat peripheral neuropathy, facial paralysis, and other conditions resulting from nerve damage or weakness, and to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected areas.
preparations.preparation_overview: Gelsemium sempervirens ointment is used to treat neuralgia, rheumatism, lumbago, paralysis, tremors, and epilepsy. It's a common preparation, widely used in herbal medicine. The common effect of Gelsemium sempervirens ointment is its ability to relieve pain and inflammation. The ointment is made from the roots and rhizomes of the plant. Preparation of Gelsemium sempervirens ointment is moderately difficult due to the need for precise extraction and processing of the plant materials.
preparation_recipe: Harvest fresh Gelsemium sempervirens roots and rhizomes, ensuring they are free of contaminants.
Clean and dry the roots and rhizomes thoroughly, then chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped roots and rhizomes in a clean glass jar and cover them with a neutral carrier oil, such as jojoba or grapeseed oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for several weeks, shaking the jar regularly to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and storing the herbal ointment in a cool, dark place.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Gelsemium sempervirens are experiencing nausea due to its toxic glycosides, which can lead to developing headache as a result of irritation to the nervous system. This can further cause showing fatigue and experiencing dizziness as the body struggles to process the toxins. Suffering from insomnia may also occur due to the plant's ability to affect the nervous system's ability to regulate sleep patterns, while experiencing muscle weakness can be a result of the plant's neurotoxic effects. Additionally, showing tremors may be a sign of more severe poisoning, which can eventually lead to experiencing confusion and developing seizures as the nervous system is severely impaired. If left unchecked, this can progress to experiencing respiratory depression, a life-threatening condition that requires immediate medical attention.

precautions.precaution_list_description: Administered by healthcare professionals to minimize risks of adverse effects and interactions.
Initial dosages of Gelsemium sempervirens should be small and gradually increased to assess individual tolerance.
Consecutive administration of high doses can cause respiratory failure and death.
Frequent measurement of pulse, breathing rate, and blood pressure to detect signs of Gelsemium sempervirens poisoning.
Some people experience skin irritation, eye redness, and respiratory problems after handling Gelsemium sempervirens.
Gelsemium sempervirens interacts with Ephedra sinica, Ephedra intermedia, and Ephedra sinica's analogs, increasing cardiovascular risk.
Gelsemium sempervirens is contraindicated in pregnancy and breastfeeding due to the lack of available data and the potential risk of toxicity.
Gelsemium sempervirens is toxic to children and elderly due to accumulation of toxic compounds.
Gelsemium sempervirens should be stored in an environment with a temperature range of 40-70°F (4-21°C) and humidity below 60%
Inform healthcare providers about Gelsemium sempervirens use before surgery or anesthesia due to potential interactions.

properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Gelsemium sempervirens are attributed to its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, which exhibit antimicrobial activity, inhibiting the growth of various pathogens. The plant's antioxidant properties are due to the presence of phenolic compounds, which protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Additionally, the volatile oils in Gelsemium sempervirens possess antispasmodic properties, helping to relieve muscle spasms and cramps. The analgesic properties of the plant are attributed to the presence of saponins, which have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation. The bioactive compounds in Gelsemium sempervirens also exhibit neuroprotective, muscle relaxant, and anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to its use in the treatment of various health conditions, including pain relief, and the prevention of muscle wasting and inflammation.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Gelsemium sempervirens are gelsemine which is a major alkaloid responsible for its toxic effects, saponins which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, flavonoids such as kaempferol and quercetin which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, alkaloids like gelsemine and gelsevirensic acid which contribute to its sedative and analgesic effects, and phenolic acids including caffeic and ferulic acids which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The combination of these constituents is believed to contribute to the plant's reported therapeutic effects, including its use in traditional medicine for the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other conditions. The specific mechanisms by which these constituents exert their effects are not fully understood, but research suggests that they may work together to produce a range of biological activities. Studies have shown that the flavonoids and phenolic acids in Gelsemium sempervirens can scavenge free radicals and inhibit the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes, while the alkaloids may contribute to its sedative and analgesic effects by interacting with the nervous system. Overall, the healing constituents of Gelsemium sempervirens appear to work together to produce a range of therapeutic effects, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Gelsemium sempervirens for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are highly concentrated extracts used for their antispasmodic, sedative, and anti-inflammatory properties to treat anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms. A salve, typically made with a combination of tincture and other natural ingredients, is applied topically to relieve pain and reduce inflammation in the muscles and joints. Gelsemium tea is often consumed to alleviate symptoms of stress, fatigue, and restlessness, while a decoction of the plant is used as an infusion to treat fever and rheumatic conditions. An ointment made from the plant's roots is applied directly to the affected area to soothe pain and inflammation, often in the case of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. These preparations are typically used under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as Gelsemium sempervirens can have adverse interactions with other medications and may cause side effects.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Gelsemium sempervirens medicinally are to use it under medical supervision due to its potential toxicity. Start with low doses to assess individual sensitivity, and avoid overuse to prevent accumulation of its toxic compounds, including gelsemine. Monitoring vital signs, such as heart rate and blood pressure, is essential to detect any adverse effects. Be aware of sensitivities, especially in individuals with pre-existing cardiovascular conditions, as gelsemine can exacerbate arrhythmias. Additionally, disclose the use of Gelsemium sempervirens to healthcare providers, especially when taking other herbs that may interact with it, such as sedatives, and avoid it in children and elderly, store in a cool, dry place, and exercise caution during pregnancy and breastfeeding.
intro_description: Gelsemium sempervirens, commonly known as Carolina yellow jessamine, is a medicinal herb used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, depression, insomnia, and neurological disorders such as Parkinson's disease and multiple sclerosis. The herb has been found to have several health benefits, including its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of chronic fatigue syndrome. Gelsemium sempervirens possesses therapeutic properties such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, which contribute to its medicinal value. The bioactive compounds present in the herb include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. Traditionally, people make a herbal preparation known as Gelsemium tincture or extract, which is often used to treat various health conditions and is also used in homeopathic medicine.
